hyp.name: VisualServo-TTA: detector-anchored test-time displacement consistency on the named target
hyp.bold_idea: At inference, run an open-vocab detector on the CURRENT RGB to find the named object, back-project its pixel to a world-frame ray, and adapt a few warm parameters per step so the policy's net commanded eef displacement points at the DETECTED object (not the memorized location). The memorizer satisfies this on clean frames but violates it exactly when positions are swapped, creating a gradient only off-distribution.
hyp.math:
Notation: at step k, eef world position p_k = obs["robot0_eef_pos"] (3D, real proprio). Open-vocab detector D (OWL-ViT / Grounding-DINO on the agentview RGB I_k) returns the bbox center pixel u* of the noun phrase parsed from the instruction (the target object). The agentview camera is STATIC; its projection P=K[R|t] is recovered DATA-FREE once per episode by least-squares fitting the robot's OWN eef across the first M warmup frames: we already know p_k (proprio) and we can detect the gripper pixel g_k by the same detector with prompt "robot gripper", giving correspondences (p_k -> g_k) solved for P (PnP, >=6 points, M~8 steps the policy is idle/stabilizing anyway -- num_steps_wait). Back-project u* through P to a world ray r(λ)=c+λd (c=camera center, d=unit ray dir). The target's lateral world direction from the eef is t_k = proj_⊥(closest point on r to the table plane z=z_table) - p_k, a unit vector in the table plane; only its xy direction is trusted (depth-free).
Predicted action chunk a = head(z) ∈ R^{8x7}; net commanded translation Δ = Σ_{i=1..8} a[i,0:3] (delta-eef chunk sums to the displacement the policy intends). Define the detector-anchored test-time loss on the UNLABELED test frame:
  L_align(θ) = 1 - cos( Δ_xy , t_k,xy )            (point the motion at the seen object)
  L_consist(θ) = || Δ(I_k) - Δ(aug(I_k)) ||_1      (TENT-free self-consistency: small photometric/crop jitter must not move the target-directed displacement)
  L_tta = L_align + β L_consist,  with a gate ω = sigmoid((cos-margin) ... ) only applied when the detector confidence > τ AND |Δ_xy| > ε (so a stationary/grasp phase is not penalized).
Adapt θ_tta = {action_queries embedding + the LayerNorm/affine (γ,β) and gating_factor scalars inside the action head's MLPResNet blocks + the zero-init TargetLocalizer.target_to_query} by n_in∈{1..3} inner SGD steps per requery, starting EVERY requery from the frozen warm checkpoint θ0 (episodic reset, no drift). Update: θ_tta ← θ0 - η ∇_θ L_tta(I_k, instruction). All other weights frozen. The unnormalize map is bf16-exact so Δ in normalized space maps monotonically to world delta; align is computed in normalized units after applying the fixed per-dim scale from norm_stats so xy directions are comparable.
hyp.implementation: Pure inference-side, no retrain. (1) In experiments/robot/libero/run_libero_eval.py: during the existing num_steps_wait idle loop, collect (p_k, gripper-pixel) pairs and solve P via cv2.solvePnP (data-free camera calib from the robot's own motion; cache per env). (2) Add a TTA wrapper around model.predict_action in experiments/robot/openvla_utils.py::get_vla_action: parse the target noun from task_label (frozen — split on verb, or one frozen-VLM call), run detector D once per requery on obs['full_image'] to get u* and confidence, back-project to t_k using p_k=obs['state'][0:3]. (3) Re-expose the action head so it runs WITH grad on a clone of θ_tta: drop torch.inference_mode() for the n_in inner steps (use torch.enable_grad on just the head + action_queries + target_to_query; LLM/vision stay frozen and detached — cheap, ~0.5B and only the small head backprops). Compute L_tta from Δ=Σ predicted a[:,0:3], step Adam/SGD n_in times, reset to θ0 after emitting the chunk. (4) Detector = OWL-ViT-base (transformers, off-the-shelf, real-RGB-transferable) loaded once; ~30ms/frame on H100. (5) Hooks already present: predict_action returns the chunk; obs['state'] gives proprio eef; full_image gives RGB. No grasp_target/sim GT used at test time. Ablate n_in, β, τ; battery = LIBERO-PRO pos/obj/sem/task/env + clean + ImageNet-C.
hyp.why_generalizes: The training-fit trap is about a memorizing solution already satisfying any constraint evaluated on TRAINING points. L_tta is never evaluated at training time and never sees training labels — it is a fresh constraint computed from the actual test image and the actual detected object. On a clean episode the named object sits where the policy memorized it, so Δ already points at it: cos≈1, gradient≈0, clean is held by construction (this is why it cannot regress clean=1.00). On a SWAPPED episode the object is at the new (unseen) location while the memorizer drives Δ toward the old empty spot: cos is low, ∇L_align is large and points the commanded displacement toward the truly-visible object. The signal that moves UNSEEN swap positions is the detector firing on the real current pixel — a per-position, position-conditioned target that the absolute-position memorizer structurally cannot anticipate, because the detector reads the scene rather than the learned prior. Generalization is to ANY swap layout, not specific training points, since t_k is recomputed live for whatever configuration the camera shows. L_consist additionally regularizes so the adapted policy stays a function of the image, not a degenerate shortcut.

vet.fatal_flaw: Two real (fixable) flaws, neither fatal. (1) CALIBRATION ILL-CONDITIONING: the proposal solves the camera projection P via cv2.solvePnP from eef->gripper-pixel correspondences collected during the num_steps_wait idle loop. But verified in run_libero_eval.py the robot runs get_libero_dummy_action (zeros) for those ~10 steps -> the eef is essentially STATIONARY, so the 3D points span a near-zero baseline and PnP is rank-deficient. The very window the proposal calls 'idle anyway' is exactly what breaks the calibration. (2) UNCONSTRAINED HEAD ADAPTATION corrupting grasp: L_align only constrains cos(Delta_xy, t_k_xy)=1; it pins neither z-descent, rotation, gripper timing, nor magnitude. A few inner-SGD steps on the 24-block MLPResNet can rotate Delta_xy correctly yet break the z/gripper schedule -> reach-but-no-grasp, which can leave swap near zero. L_consist (image self-consistency) does not protect against this. NOT a trap-failure though: the test-time gradient is genuinely off-distribution (detector reads the live swapped pixel; memorizer points Delta at the old empty spot -> non-zero corrective ∇L_align only off-distribution, ~0 on clean so clean=1.00 held by construction) -- categorically unlike the nulled H1/H2/H4/H5 training-data constraints. Confirmed hooks all exist: obs['state'][0:3]=eef_pos, obs['full_image']=static agentview RGB, predict_action returns the 8x7 chunk, action[:,0:3]=delta-eef under LIBERO OSC_POSE, and the named adapt params (action_queries embedding, MLPResNet LN affines, gating_factor scalars zero-init, target_to_query zero-init) are all present.
vet.fix: (1) Drop per-episode PnP-from-warmup. The agentview camera is STATIC and FIXED across every episode of a suite, so calibrate the projection P ONCE, offline, from a single full rollout where the eef sweeps a real 3D trajectory (well-conditioned PnP), and cache it -- still data-free/real-transferable (real robots do hand-eye calibration exactly this way from proprio+detected gripper). For de-risking the LIBERO experiment only, you may read MuJoCo's fixed agentview camera matrix at env build (eval-time scene geometry, NOT a privileged object GT pose, so it does not violate the no-sim-GT rule), but keep the PnP path as the real-transferable method of record. (2) Add cheap by-construction guards so head adaptation cannot wreck the grasp: (a) freeze the gripper and z (action[:,2], action[:,6]) dims during the inner loop -- adapt ONLY the xy-bearing-relevant params; (b) add an anchor term lambda*||Delta(theta_tta)-Delta(theta0)||_2 on the non-xy action dims so descent/gripper schedule stays at the warm-start prediction while only the lateral bearing rotates; (c) keep n_in in {1,2}, small eta, and the (already-specified) episodic reset to theta0 every requery to prevent drift. Validate on a held-out split, never tune tau/beta/eta on the LIBERO-PRO test set (CLAUDE.md rule 3). Report full battery (obj/pos/sem/task/env + clean + ImageNet-C) per /exp-record.

spec:
I now have full confirmation of every hook. I have enough to deliver the decisive ranking and build-ready specs.

---

# RANKING & BUILD SPEC: moving LIBERO-PRO position-swap off 0.00

## Verdict on the survivor set

Only one survivor was passed in (VisualServo-TTA). It genuinely escapes the training-fit trap (vet-confirmed, escape-route A). But a single test-time-only shot is fragile (the vet found two real flaws). Per your "be decisive, top 2-3" instruction, I ranked it against the two strongest *latent* candidates implied by the escape taxonomy that I verified are buildable in THIS codebase — a route-C structural reparametrization and a route-D data-free off-distribution constructor. All three escape the trap; they differ on math-soundness and swap-likelihood.

Codebase facts I verified (all load-bearing):
- `obs["state"][0:3] = robot0_eef_pos` real proprio; `obs["full_image"]` = STATIC agentview RGB (`get_libero_image`). Confirmed in `prepare_observation` (run_libero_eval.py:307-313).
- The idle loop runs `get_libero_dummy_action` (zeros) for `num_steps_wait=10` steps → eef essentially stationary → **per-episode PnP is rank-deficient** (vet flaw #1 confirmed at run_libero_eval.py:373-376).
- `predict_action` returns `(action[8,7], actions_hidden_states)`; action chunk is delta-eef under OSC_POSE; `action[:,0:3]`=Δxyz, `action[:,6]`=gripper (modeling_prismatic.py:1118-1120, 1019).
- Adapt params all present and zero/small-init: `action_queries` (485-486), `MLPResNetBlock_Pro.gating_factor` zero-init (action_heads.py:320), block LayerNorm affines, `TargetLocalizer.target_to_query` zero-init (target_localizer.py:50-52).
- `inference_mode()` wraps the whole `get_vla_action` (openvla_utils.py:776) — must be dropped for the head-only inner loop.
- `grasp_target` is a TRAIN-time signal (finetune_fused.py:511-548); MUST NOT be used at test time. Specs below honor that.
- LIBERO-PRO swap suite = same objects, swapped positions; instruction unchanged (run_libero_eval.py:73-83). So the named-noun parse is identical clean vs swap — the ONLY thing that changes is where the detector fires. This is exactly the position-conditioned test signal.

---

## RANK 1 — BUILD THIS FIRST: VisualServo-TTA (detector-anchored test-time bearing alignment)

**Why first:** It is the only candidate that is *purely inference-side* (zero retrain, zero risk to clean=1.00 by construction: on clean frames cos≈1 ⇒ ∇≈0), and the test signal (open-vocab detector firing on the live swapped pixel) is precisely a per-position target the absolute-position memorizer structurally cannot anticipate. It is the boldest *defensible* shot because the corrective gradient exists **only** off-distribution. Both vet flaws are cheaply fixable. Lowest blast radius, fastest to a swap≠0 signal.

### Mechanism (apply both vet fixes)

**Camera calibration — REPLACE per-episode PnP (vet fix #1).** Calibrate the static agentview projection `P=K[R|t]` ONCE per suite, offline, two ways:
- *Method of record (real-transferable):* run a single throwaway rollout where the policy actually moves the arm through a 3D sweep; log `(eef_pos p_k, detected gripper pixel g_k)` for ~40 well-separated frames; `cv2.solvePnP` (RANSAC) → cache `P` to disk. This is exactly real-robot hand-eye calibration from proprio + detected gripper. Conditioning is fine because the eef now spans a real baseline (unlike the idle loop).
- *De-risk shortcut (allowed, NOT sim-GT):* read MuJoCo's fixed agentview camera intrinsics/extrinsics at env build via `env.sim.model.cam_*` — this is eval-time *scene geometry*, not a privileged object pose, so it does not violate the no-sim-GT rule. Use it to validate the PnP path, then report PnP as the method of record.

**Per-step bearing target.** At each requery: parse the target noun from `task_description` (split on the verb; one frozen-VLM call optional but a regex split suffices for LIBERO templates). Run OWL-ViT-base once on `obs["full_image"]` → bbox-center pixel `u*` + confidence. Back-project `u*` through `P` to a world ray; intersect with the table plane `z=z_table` (z_table = a constant per suite, read once from the initial eef-rest or scene) → world point `w*`. Bearing `t_k = (w* − p_k)`, keep **xy direction only**, unit-normalize: `t̂_xy`. Depth-free, so only lateral direction is trusted.

**Test-time loss (computed on the live test frame; never sees train labels):**
- `Δ = Σ_{i=1..8} a[i,0:3]` = net commanded translation from the predicted chunk.
- `L_align = 1 − cos(Δ_xy, t̂_xy)` — point the motion at the *seen* object.
- `L_consist = ‖Δ(I_k) − Δ(aug(I_k))‖₁` with small crop/photometric jitter — keep the policy a function of the image.
- Gate `ω`: apply only when `detector_conf > τ` AND `‖Δ_xy‖ > ε` (skip grasp/lift/stationary phases). `L_tta = ω·(L_align + β·L_consist)`.

**By-construction grasp guards (vet fix #2) — critical so a rotated bearing doesn't wreck z/gripper:**
- During the inner loop, **freeze the z and gripper action dims** — adapt only params that move lateral bearing. Concretely: compute `L_tta` only from `Δ_xy`; add anchor `λ·‖Δ_{z,rot,grip}(θ_tta) − Δ_{z,rot,grip}(θ₀)‖₂` pinning the descent/rotation/gripper schedule to the warm prediction.
- Adapt set `θ_tta = {action_queries.weight, every MLPResNetBlock_Pro.gating_factor + its ffn LayerNorm affines, target_to_query}`. Small `η`, `n_in∈{1,2}`, Adam.
- **Episodic reset:** restore `θ_tta ← θ₀` after every requery (no drift). θ₀ = frozen warm checkpoint.

### Exact codebase hooks
1. `experiments/robot/libero/run_libero_eval.py`: in `run_episode`, after the idle loop, do offline-PnP-load once per `task_suite` (cache by suite name). Pass cached `P`, `z_table` into `get_action`.
2. `experiments/robot/openvla_utils.py::get_vla_action`: wrap `vla.predict_action`. **Drop the outer `torch.inference_mode()`** (line 776); wrap the frozen LLM+vision forward in `torch.no_grad()` and detach `multi_layer_hidden_states`, then run ONLY `action_head.predict_action` + `action_queries` + `target_to_query` under `enable_grad` on a clone of `θ_tta`. Backprop touches only the ~0.5B head — cheap. Step Adam `n_in` times, emit chunk, reset.
3. OWL-ViT-base loaded once (transformers, real-RGB-transferable, ~30 ms/frame H100).

### Eval expectation
Clean held at 1.00 **by construction** (cos≈1 ⇒ ∇≈0 on clean). Swap: expect a real move off 0.00 — magnitude bounded by how often the gripper currently reaches the *wrong* xy spot (L_align rotates it to the right column). With the z/gripper anchor preventing reach-but-no-grasp, I'd target swap into a non-trivial band rather than full recovery from a single shot. Full battery: obj/pos/sem/task/env + clean + ImageNet-C; ≥3 seeds; never tune τ/β/η/λ on the PRO test set (hold-out split per CLAUDE.md rule 3).

---

## RANK 2 — BUILD SECOND (highest *ceiling* if Rank 1 plateaus): Object-Centric Action Reframe (route C, by-construction)

**Idea:** Factor absolute position out of the action *representation* so the network has no absolute coordinate to memorize. Reparametrize the predicted chunk into the **detector-anchored relative frame**: instead of regressing world Δeef, regress displacement **relative to the named object's current image-back-projected xy bearing**. At train time `grasp_target` (already in the batch, finetune_fused.py:515) gives the object frame for free — supervise the head in `(eef − object)`-relative coordinates. At test time the same frame is reconstructed live from the detector (no grasp_target needed). Because the head only ever sees object-relative targets, a position-memorizing solution is **structurally unavailable** — swap just relocates the frame origin, which the detector tracks.

**Why second not first:** It is route-C "by construction" (strongest *kind* of fix, highest swap ceiling) but requires a warm-start fine-tune and touches `run_forward_pass` + the head's coordinate convention + the inference unprojection — bigger blast radius, and it must be shown not to dent clean. Build it once Rank 1 proves the detector bearing signal is reliable on swap (Rank 1 de-risks the exact detector/PnP stack Rank 2 depends on).

**Hooks:** `run_forward_pass` (finetune_fused.py:314) — recompute the regression target in the object-relative frame using `batch["grasp_target"]`; `predict_action` — add object origin back at inference using the detector bearing; reuse Rank 1's `P`/OWL-ViT calibration verbatim.

**Eval expectation:** highest potential swap recovery of the three (absolute position is gone by construction), but clean must be re-verified (frame reconstruction adds a dependency on detector reliability even on clean).

---

## RANK 3 — fallback (route D, data-free off-distribution): Detector-Composited Swap Augmentation

**Idea:** Construct genuinely off-distribution positions data-free by **compositing** existing training frames: detect each object with OWL-ViT, cut-paste two objects to swapped pixels (Poisson-blend), and recompute `grasp_target` from the new pixel via the calibrated `P`. This manufactures the unseen swap layouts the memorizer never saw, then a short fine-tune forces generalization. Data-free (no teleop, no sim re-render), real-transferable (detector + RGB only).

**Why third:** It is a *retrain* whose validity hinges entirely on composite realism — blending artifacts can teach a shortcut, and it reintroduces a training-data constraint (mitigated only because the data is now genuinely off-distribution). Highest engineering risk-per-payoff. Keep as fallback if both test-time (Rank 1) and reframe (Rank 2) under-deliver.

---

## Decision

**Build VisualServo-TTA (Rank 1) FIRST.** It is the only zero-retrain shot, cannot regress clean by construction, isolates the single most promising real-transferable signal (live detector bearing on the swapped pixel), and de-risks the exact calibration/detector stack that Ranks 2 and 3 both reuse. Apply both vet fixes verbatim: (1) offline once-per-suite PnP (or MuJoCo cam matrix to de-risk), never per-episode; (2) freeze z/gripper dims + anchor the non-xy schedule to θ₀, n_in∈{1,2}, episodic reset. If swap moves off 0.00 but plateaus from reach-but-no-grasp residue, escalate to Rank 2 (object-centric reframe) which removes absolute position by construction.

Key files: `/mnt/data/sftp/data/quangpt3/gib-vla/StableVLA/experiments/robot/openvla_utils.py` (TTA wrapper, drop inference_mode at line 776), `/mnt/data/sftp/data/quangpt3/gib-vla/StableVLA/experiments/robot/libero/run_libero_eval.py` (PnP calib + plumb P into get_action, lines 333-409), `/mnt/data/sftp/data/quangpt3/gib-vla/StableVLA/prismatic/models/action_heads.py` (adapt gating_factor/LN params), `/mnt/data/sftp/data/quangpt3/gib-vla/StableVLA/prismatic/util/target_localizer.py` (target_to_query), `/mnt/data/sftp/data/quangpt3/gib-vla/StableVLA/vla-scripts/finetune_fused.py:314,515` (Rank 2/3 reframe + grasp_target frame).
